compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
Jira reconciler silently fails to sync comments and inbound links while reporting
`converged` — three distinct defects, all hidden behind a pass that counts failed
sub-operations toward "applied N of N" and exits 0.

## Context

Investigation (/rebar-debug, 2026-07-17) established that status/existence sync is
healthy (0 drift across ~1200 tickets), but three field-level sync paths are broken
and invisible:

- **Comment sync (P1):** `get_comments()` returns only the first 50 of N comments
  (no `--paginate`), so outbound dedup re-posts the rest every pass. 13 tickets have
  been inflated to Jira's 5000-comment hard cap (local truth: 22-76 comments each);
  further adds fail `COMMENTS_PER_ISSUE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` and are swallowed.
- **Inbound link direction (P2):** `_diff_links_inbound` has its inward/outward
  branches swapped (`blocks`<->`depends_on`). Latent since 2026-06-15 (`f6687adf4`);
  UNMASKED when the outbound direction fix landed (`6b25dbead`/`43d154523`, 07-16/17)
  — the two inversions previously cancelled on round-trip. Now ~815 inbound link
  applies/pass are cycle-rejected by rebar's guard.
- **Observability (meta):** a pass with failed sub-ops still prints `applied N of N`
  / exits 0 / logs `converged`. The `RECONCILER_FAIL_SILENT_NOOP` flag (default off)
  exists but is not enabled.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] P1: outbound comment sync is idempotent — a steady-state pass emits 0 comment
      adds for a ticket already mirrored, even when the Jira side has >50 comments.
- [ ] P2: an inbound Jira `Blocks` link round-trips to the correct rebar relation
      (`blocks`/`depends_on`) with NO cycle-rejection against the correct local edge.
- [ ] Observability: a pass with any silent sub-op no-op reports failure (non-zero
      exit) rather than `converged`, once P1/P2 remove the standing noise.
- [ ] Test hardening: unit + integration + E2E coverage added for all three failure
      modes AND same-kind gaps; the new tests fail on the pre-fix code.
- [ ] Jira data cleanup: the 13 capped tickets are de-duplicated so new comments can
      sync again (executed AFTER P1 is deployed to main).
- [ ] All child work landed on `main` through Gerrit.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All child tickets (P1, P2, observability, test-hardening, Jira-cleanup) are closed.
- [ ] `rebar reconcile --mode reconcile-check` (or a live pass) shows 0 comment-cap
      failures and 0 inbound link cycle-rejections for the previously-affected tickets.
compiled_state.title: Jira reconciler: silent comment + inbound-link sync failures
